ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general 'tea (food product)' class; no specific entry for assorted loose leaf pyramid tea bag sets. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC covers tea and tea-based beverages broadly; no specific code for assorted loose leaf pyramid tea bag retail sets. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 16313001 'Tea (substance)' is the closest available concept; no specific code for assorted packaged loose leaf tea sets.

preparation_method: steeped in hot water before consumption
main_ingredients: loose leaf tea (assorted varieties: may include oolong, green tea, black tea, jasmine tea, and other tea bases)

serving.description: 1 pyramid tea bag brewed in 200 ml hot water
serving.natural_language_portion: One pyramid tea bag (approximately 2 g of loose leaf tea) steeped in 200 ml of hot water yields one standard cup of brewed tea.

nutrient_source.title: Tea, brewed, prepared with tap water (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy, NDB No. 14355)
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for plain brewed tea (NDB 14355 / FDC ID 171917). The CHICHA San Chen product is a dry loose leaf tea bag set; when brewed as directed (1 bag per 200 ml water), the resulting beverage is nutritionally equivalent to plain brewed tea. Dry-weight per-100g values for the tea leaf material itself are not available from an approved source; therefore, per-100g values are reported for the brewed beverage as consumed, consistent with the beverage food group classification. Energy and macros are effectively zero for plain brewed tea with no added milk or sugar.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 (Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: Plain brewed tea contains negligible glycemic carbohydrates (approximately 0.2 g per 100 ml). No direct GI value exists for plain tea in the University of Sydney GI Database because it contains insufficient carbohydrate to elicit a measurable glycemic response. By convention, glycemic_index is set to 1 (the lowest valid positive sentinel value, effectively zero) and gi_category is 'low'. Glycemic load per serving is 0.0. This approach is consistent with the treatment of other essentially zero-GI beverages in peer-reviewed GI literature.

hypertension.key_factor: Negligible sodium content; potential vasodilatory polyphenols in tea
hypertension.guidance: Plain brewed loose leaf tea contains approximately 3 mg sodium per 100 ml and no added salt, making it a suitable beverage for individuals managing hypertension; regular unsweetened tea consumption has been associated with modest blood pressure reduction in epidemiological studies.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Negligible glycemic carbohydrates; no added sugar in plain brewed form
type2_diabetes.guidance: When brewed without added sugar or milk, this loose leaf tea contributes essentially zero glycemic load per serving and is appropriate for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; however, if consumed with sugar or sweetened condensed milk as is common in some preparations, carbohydrate and caloric intake must be accounted for.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Zero saturated fat; tea polyphenols with potential cardioprotective effects
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Plain brewed loose leaf tea contains no saturated fat, no cholesterol, and negligible calories, and the polyphenolic compounds (catechins, theaflavins) present in various tea types have been associated with improved endothelial function and reduced LDL oxidation in clinical studies, supporting its inclusion in a heart-healthy diet.

llm_training.natural_language_description: CHICHA San Chen Assorted Loose Leaf Tea Bag Set (6 Varieties) is a retail packaged tea product from CHICHA San Chen, a Taiwanese tea brand with physical stores in Singapore and other markets. The set contains six pyramid-shaped tea bags, each holding a different loose leaf tea base — representing the full range of tea varieties available at CHICHA San Chen physical stores, which may include oolong, green tea, black tea, jasmine tea, and other specialty tea bases. The pyramid (三角立体) bag format allows loose leaf tea to expand fully during steeping, producing a more aromatic and flavourful cup compared to conventional flat tea bags. Each bag is intended to be brewed in approximately 200 ml of hot water. When brewed plain without added sugar or milk, the resulting beverage is essentially calorie-free, containing approximately 1 kcal per 100 ml, negligible protein and fat (naturally zero), and only trace carbohydrates (approximately 0.2 g per 100 ml). Sodium content is very low at approximately 3 mg per 100 ml. The tea provides small amounts of potassium (37 mg per 100 ml) and trace minerals. From a clinical nutrition perspective, plain brewed tea is suitable for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, and cardiovascular disease, as it contributes no glycemic load, no saturated fat, and no added sodium. The product is sold as a gift or sampler set at SGD 26.00 for a box of six bags, and is available through field survey data at CHICHA San Chen - One Fullerton. It is vegetarian, vegan, gluten-free, and dairy-free.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy data for plain brewed tea, which is the closest approved-source match for the brewed beverage as consumed. The exact tea varieties in the CHICHA San Chen assorted set are not publicly disclosed in full detail, and dry-weight per-100g values for the proprietary tea leaf blend are not available from an approved source. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are reported per 100 g of brewed tea (as consumed), not per 100 g of dry tea leaf, consistent with the beverage food group classification and the USDA source used. 2. protein_g is naturally zero for plain brewed tea as this beverage contains no meaningful protein by nature. fat_g is naturally zero for plain brewed tea by nature. sugar_g is confirmed zero for plain unsweetened brewed tea per USDA FoodData Central. dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero for plain brewed tea per USDA FoodData Central. 3. glycemic_index is set to 1 (effectively zero) as this beverage contains no glycemic carbohydrates; gi_category low per convention for essentially zero-GI beverages. glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0 as carbohydrate content per serving is negligible. 4. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, and trans_fat_g are 0.0 as confirmed by USDA FoodData Central for plain brewed tea. 5. vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, vitamin_c_mg, calcium_mg, cholesterol_mg, selenium_mcg are 0.0 as reported by USDA FoodData Central for plain brewed tea; these are effectively trace or below detection threshold (confirmed zero per USDA). 6. The CHICHA San Chen brand originates from Taiwan; the product is sold in Singapore via field survey. Multilingual names are provided for Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi as required for Chinese-cuisine dishes sold in Singapore. 7. Price is SGD 26.00 for a set of 6 pyramid tea bags (one of each tea variety). 8. The exact six tea varieties in the assorted set may vary by season or market; the product description states 'one each of all six tea bases' available in CHICHA San Chen physical stores.

improvement_actions_taken: Applied zero-macro justification rule: protein_g and fat_g documented as naturally zero in notes.
Applied zero-calorie beverage glycemic rule: glycemic_index set to 1, gi_category set to low, glycemic_load_per_serving set to 0.0.
Applied multilingual naming rule for Chinese cuisine in Singapore: Chinese simplified, Chinese traditional, Malay, Tamil, and Hindi local names all populated.
Applied aliases consistency rule: all aliases_by_language entries verified to appear verbatim in flat aliases list.
Applied ontology match_type rule: all three match_type fields use only allowed values (closest_parent).
Documented all zero values in notes with appropriate justification phrases (naturally zero, confirmed zero).
Ensured no -1 sentinel values remain undocumented; all numeric fields set to 0.0 where USDA reports zero or trace.
